name: Chicken Tandoori #1 of 2
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Ginger root, fresh and chopped
recipeInstructions:
----------------FOR LOVELY VERMILLION COLOR----------------
Mix yellow food coloring and red food coloring together.
Using a pastry brush, paint the chicken legs. Coloring will stain fingers, so be careful. Let dry.
-----------------------FOR MARINADE-----------------------
1. Remove skin from chicken legs.
2. Prick the chicken all over with a fork or thin skewer.
3. Make diagonal slashes, 1/2-inch deep, about 1 inch apart on the meat.
4. In a bowl, mix yogurt, garlic, ginger, roasted and ground cumin seeds, cardamom, red cayenne pepper, and oil (ghee or vegetable).
5. Add meat tenderizer and lemon juice to the chicken mixture, rubbing them into the slashes and all over for 2 minutes.
6. Cover and marinate at room temperature for 1/2 hour or refrigerate overnight, turning several times.
ABOUT GHEE: Ghee is clarified butter. A mixture of half margarine and salad oil can be used.
Chicken should not remain in the marinade for more than 2 days, as the meat tenderizer may alter the texture to very soft and doughy.
